feature: Phase 726: Full Truth And UX Audit Baseline
verifyScript: pnpm audit:ui-estate:runtime && pnpm audit:ui-estate:truth && pnpm audit:ui-estate:live-p1 && pnpm audit:ui-estate:live-p1-followup
notes: Built a runtime UI inventory, audit checklist, and evidence-seeded truth matrix for the current VistA-Evolved surface area.
Current truth matrix totals: 131 surfaces, with 29 P1 surfaces, 13 required-with-strong-signals, 13 required-needs-live-verification, and 3 required-unmapped.
Added a repeatable live VEHU baseline verifier that writes to artifacts/phase726-p1-live-baseline.json and artifacts/phase726-p1-live-baseline.md.
Fixed GET /vista/default-patient-list so it now returns rpcUsed: ORQPT DEFAULT PATIENT LIST, which cleared the last failing live-baseline check.
Current live chart baseline is clean: 11 checks run, 11 passing, 0 failing, using PROGRAMMER,ONE against VEHU DFN 46.

Added a second verifier for unresolved P1 surfaces so inbox, messaging, nursing, handoff, inpatient, and admin VistA pages can be classified with route-level evidence instead of remaining narrative follow-up work.
Fixed the follow-up verifier contract to match the actual nursing route payload shape (items, not notes/tasks), clearing the last two false failures.
Current unresolved-P1 follow-up baseline is clean: 16 checks run, 16 passing, 0 failing, 0 skipped, with ward candidate 12 derived from live VistA ward data.
Fixed the browser-blocking patient-search contract mismatch by returning both results and patients from GET /vista/default-patient-list.

Completed an initial live browser control audit for inbox, messages, nursing, handoff, inpatient census, inpatient bedboard, and admin VistA dashboard; findings are recorded in artifacts/phase726-p1-browser-control-audit.md.

Patient search is now browser-proven on the canonical stack: searching ZZZRETFOURNINETYFOUR rendered live ORWPT LIST ALL matches, selecting DFN 46 enabled Open Chart, and the control navigated into /cprs/chart/46/cover with the correct patient header.

Admin VistA clinics is now browser-proven on the canonical stack: the page rendered live VE CLIN LIST clinic rows, the Appointment Types tab switched into a populated secondary table, and the React tab-style warning exposed during that switch is now fixed in apps/web/src/app/cprs/admin/vista/clinics/page.tsx.

Admin VistA users is now browser-proven on the canonical stack: the page rendered live VE USER LIST user rows, the Security Keys tab switched into populated live key rows, the Menus tab search returned live OR* entries from VE MENU LIST, and the parallel React tab-style warning is now fixed in apps/web/src/app/cprs/admin/vista/users/page.tsx.

Admin VistA system is now browser-proven on the canonical stack: the page rendered live VE SYS STATUS cards, a populated VE TASKMAN LIST table, a truthful empty VE ERROR TRAP state, and a filterable VE PARAM LIST table, and the same tab-style warning pattern is now fixed in apps/web/src/app/cprs/admin/vista/system/page.tsx.

Admin VistA wards is now browser-proven on the canonical stack: the page rendered live VE WARD LIST rows, the row-detail interaction opened live VE WARD DETAIL data, the Census tab rendered live VE CENSUS rows, and the census occupancy NaN% defect plus the same tab-style warning pattern are now fixed in apps/web/src/app/cprs/admin/vista/wards/page.tsx.

Admin VistA facilities is now browser-proven on the canonical stack: the page rendered live institutions, divisions, services, stop codes, specialties, and site parameters from VE INST LIST, VE DIV LIST, VE SVC LIST, VE STOP LIST, VE SPEC LIST, and VE SITE PARM; both institutions and stop-code searches worked against live filtered rows; and the same tab-style warning pattern is now fixed in apps/web/src/app/cprs/admin/vista/facilities/page.tsx.

Admin VistA inventory exposed a real route/UI truth defect on Item Master: VE INV ITEM LIST fields were mislabeled in both the canonical admin route and the browser table, leaving NSN blank and surfacing the description under Category. That contract is now fixed in apps/api/src/routes/admin/vista-inventory.ts, apps/api/src/routes/vista-admin.ts, and apps/web/src/app/cprs/admin/vista/inventory/page.tsx, and the inventory tab-style warning pattern is fixed there as well.

Admin VistA inventory is now fully browser-proven on the canonical stack: Item Master and Vendors were corrected earlier, and the last unresolved Purchase Orders tab is now fixed in apps/api/src/routes/admin/vista-inventory.ts and apps/web/src/app/cprs/admin/vista/inventory/page.tsx to label the VE INV PO LIST payload as raw File 443 0-node fields instead of pretending opaque values are resolved vendor/date/status data.

Admin VistA clinical setup is now browser-proven on the canonical stack: Order Sets, TIU Definitions, and Health Summary render live VistA-backed data with semantically acceptable labels, while Consult Services and TIU Templates exposed real truth-label defects where raw VistA pointers and codes were presented as friendly values. Those route/UI contracts are now fixed in apps/api/src/routes/admin/vista-clinical-setup.ts and apps/web/src/app/cprs/admin/vista/clinical-setup/page.tsx by labeling the raw metadata honestly as Group IEN, Owner IEN, and Status Code, and the same tab-style warning pattern is fixed there as well.

Admin VistA lab is now browser-proven on the canonical stack: Tests, Collection Samples, and Urgency all render live VistA-backed data, and the verification pass exposed three real truth defects that are now fixed in apps/api/src/routes/admin/vista-lab.ts and apps/web/src/app/cprs/admin/vista/lab/page.tsx. The test list now labels raw type values as Type Code, the detail route no longer leaks the 1^OK status sentinel into the browser detail panel, collection samples label raw tube pointers honestly as Tube IEN, the urgency tab no longer advertises a fake Code column, and the same tab-style warning pattern is fixed there as well.

Admin VistA pharmacy is now browser-proven on the canonical stack: Drug Formulary, Routes, and Schedules all render live VistA-backed data, and the verification pass exposed four truth defects that are now fixed in apps/api/src/routes/admin/vista-pharmacy.ts and apps/web/src/app/cprs/admin/vista/pharmacy/page.tsx. The detail route no longer leaks the 1^OK status sentinel into the browser detail panel, raw drug-class values are labeled honestly as VA Class Code, route rows are labeled as Code plus Description instead of implied friendly names, schedule timing strings are labeled as Admin Times, and the same tab-style warning pattern is fixed there as well.

Admin VistA radiology is now browser-proven on the canonical stack: Procedures, Imaging Locations, and Division Params all render live VistA-backed data, and the verification pass exposed four truth defects that are now fixed in apps/api/src/routes/admin/vista-radiology.ts and apps/web/src/app/cprs/admin/vista/radiology/page.tsx. The detail route no longer leaks the 1^OK status sentinel into the browser detail panel, raw procedure type values are labeled honestly as Type Code, mixed classification versus location rows are labeled as Entry Type, raw File 79 pieces are labeled as file0Piece2 and file0Piece3 instead of implied friendly params, and the same tab-style warning pattern is fixed there as well.

Admin VistA quality is now browser-proven on the canonical stack: Clinical Reminders and QA Site Parameters render live VistA-backed data, and the verification pass exposed three truth defects that are now fixed in apps/api/src/routes/admin/vista-quality.ts and apps/web/src/app/cprs/admin/vista/quality/page.tsx. The detail route no longer leaks the 1^OK status sentinel into the browser detail panel, QA site params are labeled honestly as facilityCode and raw file pieces instead of implied friendly semantics, and the same tab-style warning pattern is fixed there as well.

Admin VistA workforce is now browser-proven on the canonical stack: Providers and Person Classes render live VistA-backed data, and the verification pass exposed two truth defects that are now fixed in apps/api/src/routes/admin/vista-workforce.ts and apps/web/src/app/cprs/admin/vista/workforce/page.tsx. The detail route no longer leaks the 1^OK status sentinel into the browser detail panel, provider-list person-class pointers are labeled honestly as Person Class IEN instead of Taxonomy, and the same tab-style warning pattern is fixed there as well.

Admin VistA provisioning is now browser-proven on the canonical stack: the five-step tenant wizard is backed by live /admin/provisioning catalog routes, and the browser pass exposed a real contract defect where the country dropdown hardcoded only five entries even though /admin/provisioning/country-configs returned seven. That page now binds to the live country-config catalog in apps/web/src/app/cprs/admin/vista/provisioning/page.tsx, and an end-to-end browser create flow produced persisted tenant a5797319-b3be-455f-b4f4-609b4cab7d5d for Phase 726 Audit Hospital.

Admin VistA billing is now browser-proven on the canonical stack: IB Site Parameters, Insurance Companies, and Claims Summary render live VistA-backed data, and the verification pass exposed three truth defects that are now fixed in apps/api/src/routes/admin/vista-billing-config.ts and apps/web/src/app/cprs/admin/vista/billing/page.tsx. The 1^OK sentinel no longer leaks into billing payloads, the insurance-company list headers now match the real live fields, and raw billing keys are labeled honestly as siteIen, rateTypeCode, billingClockDays, REIMBURSE_FLAG, and totalClaims.

Observed live UI proof includes inbox acknowledge interaction reducing the pending count, live MailMan baskets and message subjects, live TIU-backed nursing notes and tasks, truthful local-store handoff messaging, live inpatient census and bedboard counts, and a fully populated VistA operational dashboard.

Extended the browser control audit to /cprs/emar?dfn=46: schedule, allergy warnings, administration fallback, and BCMA verification are now covered by direct UI evidence rather than route claims only.

MailMan clinician send is now repaired and live-verified: ZVE MAIL SEND was fixed to accept nested XWB LIST params for TEXT and REC, direct POST /vista/mailman/send now returns ok:true with a live vistaRef, and browser compose/send succeeded with Message sent to VistA MailMan (144725).
Package-generated MailMan messages now reply truthfully in the browser: non-user senders show a manual-recipient warning and leave the DUZ field blank instead of fabricating a pseudo-DUZ recipient.
Deploying updated .m routines from Windows required in-container normalization to strip CRLF and the UTF-8 BOM before VEHU would compile the updated MailMan routine cleanly.

The eMAR browser pass exposed a route-level fallback defect: /emar/administer could return ok:true with missing-RPC text in noteIen after probing unavailable ZVENAS MEDLOG. Patched source now gates ZVENAS MEDLOG with the RPC capability cache and requires a numeric TIU note IEN before returning success.

Fresh live verification on a patched API instance at port 3102 now returns a truthful eMAR administration fallback payload with noteIen 14385, rpcUsed [TIU CREATE RECORD, TIU SET DOCUMENT TEXT], and explicit _note text explaining the BCMA write limitation.

Second-pass handoff verification confirmed the browser matches route truth for ward 12: ORQPT WARD PATIENTS returned 0 patients, and active/accept/archive handoff tabs all showed truthful empty states while the reports surface remained explicitly local-store-backed.

Live browser handoff lifecycle proof is now complete on populated ward 6: /handoff/ward-patients?ward=6 returned 5 VistA-backed patients, the Create tab loaded the same five patients, Create Handoff Report succeeded with report ID hoff-1773250713803-f9d8e44f, and that same report was then submitted, accepted, archived, and shown in the Archive tab in a single browser session for PROGRAMMER,ONE.

The browser pass exposed a same-user handoff accept loophole, which is now fixed: the local-store accept path rejects self-accept with 409 Cannot accept -- incoming staff must differ from report creator, and fresh live VEHU proof on a patched API instance showed second-user acceptance succeeding for TDNURSE,ONE on report hoff-1773251511877-f34362a3.
Added targeted regression coverage in apps/api/tests/handoff-store.test.ts and apps/api/tests/emar-route-helpers.test.ts; vitest passed 4/4 across handoff acceptance and eMAR note-IEN parsing guards.

Extended the browser control audit to /cprs/scheduling: clinic schedule, patient appointments, request queue, wait list, recall, lifecycle, posture, and parity are now inspected at the control level rather than left as route-only claims.

The scheduling browser pass exposed three truth defects on the canonical 3001 stack: SD W/L wait-list runtime errors were rendered as visible clinic rows, reference-data RPC runtime payloads could leak into parsed values, and /scheduling/recall?dfn=46 still used SD RECALL LIST instead of the patient-scoped recall RPC.

Patched source now gates SD W/L runtime-error payloads before parsing, uses SD RECALL LIST BY PATIENT for recall, and suppresses the sandbox RECALL LIST EMPTY sentinel so empty recall lists render truthfully.

Fresh live verification on a patched API instance at port 3106 confirmed the repaired scheduling contract: /scheduling/waitlist returned only the local request row with pending:true, /scheduling/reference-data returned empty arrays with truthful runtime_error notes, and /scheduling/recall?dfn=46 returned ok:true with data:[] and vistaGrounding for File 403.5.

After restarting the canonical API on port 3001, the browser now matches that repaired scheduling contract: Wait List no longer renders raw M error text, and Recall shows a truthful configuration-required empty state for unpopulated sandbox File 403.5.

Added targeted regression coverage in apps/api/tests/scheduling-runtime-helpers.test.ts and reran apps/api/tests/scheduling-sd.test.ts; vitest passed 20/20 across the scheduling helper guards and integration surface.

Note Builder is now browser-proven on the canonical stack: the page no longer opens with an empty template picker or invalid DFN 3, starter templates are served from new session-level routes with truthful builtin fallback, the client fetches and sends CSRF on generate, and the field-binding contract now matches the backend key/fieldType payload so generated notes contain real clinical text.

Direct live API proof for the note-builder slice now shows GET /encounter/note-builder/templates returning 50 starter templates with fallbackUsed:true, GET /encounter/note-builder/templates/:id returning full field metadata, and POST /encounter/note-builder/generate returning ok:true with nested note output for DFN 46.
Added targeted regression coverage in apps/api/tests/note-builder-template-fallback.test.ts; vitest passed 2/2 across builtin template listing and local draft generation.

The VistA workspace shell is now browser-proven on the canonical stack: package navigation and kernel status stayed live, `XM` terminal mode connected successfully, the browser terminal showed live SSH output, and interactive `pwd` returned `/home/vista`.

The ws-terminal repair is broader than the original auth fix: apps/api/src/routes/ws-terminal.ts now uses request.session, wraps terminal audit writes safely, removes the stale post-start audit crash path, and parses buffered resize/ping control frames instead of echoing JSON into the shell.

Direct authenticated WebSocket proof matched the final browser pass by returning a `connected` control frame plus binary shell payloads, and the final canonical browser run showed no leaked resize JSON in the terminal pane.

Inpatient census and bedboard are now browser-proven on the canonical stack: the authenticated browser session fetched 29 live wards from /vista/inpatient/wards, /inpatient/census rendered 29 wards and 182 total patients, clicking 7A GEN MED opened a 38-patient census table, and /inpatient/bedboard rendered the same ward as a populated 38-occupied bed grid.

The handoff create flow exposed a truth gap on the canonical browser: /handoff/ward-patients?ward=7A%20GEN%20MED already returned count 0 with pendingTargets for missing CRHD RPCs, but the UI only showed 0 patients loaded. apps/web/src/app/cprs/handoff/page.tsx now surfaces the integration-pending reason, sandbox note, pending CRHD targets, and live fallback RPC ORQPT WARD PATIENTS instead of implying an empty ward.

The canonical CPRS chart shell is now browser-proven on the VEHU stack: /cprs/chart/46/cover rendered stable patient context plus live cover widgets that matched authenticated route fetches for problems, allergies, medications, vitals, labs, appointments, and reminders, and /cprs/chart/46/notes navigated cleanly with live TIU-backed note rows.

Chart row-level interactivity is also browser-proven: clicking the first note row on /cprs/chart/46/notes opened TIU note detail for IEN 14385 with full note text and explicit grounding Source: VistA TIU, and no new chart-shell truth defect was found in this pass.

Runtime audit tracker preservation is now complete: scripts/ui-estate/build-runtime-ui-estate.mjs reads evidence-backed overrides from data/ui-estate/runtime-ui-audit-overrides.json, regenerated checklist output now reports browser-proven: 27 instead of resetting all reviewed surfaces to unreviewed, and the derived truth matrix now preserves those statuses for proven Phase 726 surfaces.

Legacy P1 alias routes are now closed correctly instead of being certified by assumption: /patient-search and /chart/:dfn/:tab were replaced with canonical redirects to the already proven CPRS routes, and live frontend verification showed NEXT_REDIRECT responses to /cprs/patient-search, /cprs/chart/46/cover, and the normalized cover-sheet alias target.

The Phase 726 integrations-console slice is now browser-proven on a healthy canonical 3001/VEHU window: apps/web/src/app/cprs/admin/integrations/page.tsx now uses the authenticated session tenant instead of hardcoded default, the registry and legacy-connectors tabs truthfully render the live zero-state for tenant 77371ffb-9929-4344-9fea-a0a7bbcc645e, the VistA HL7/HLO tab matches live interop telemetry (20 logical links, HL7.VEHU.DOMAIN.GOV, 19 apps, queue depth 7), and the message-browser drill-in opens live masked detail for HL7 message IEN 39358.

The integrations slice also closed a live UI defect in the HL7 message-detail segment summary: repeated VistA segment labels no longer reuse seg.type as a React key, so the detail table stays stable under live message data.

The Phase 726 payer-db slice is now browser-proven on the canonical 3001 stack: the page rendered the live 57-row payer registry, search reduced the table to the single live US-AETNA row, SG-AIA capability detail matched the live empty capability set, the Evidence and Audit tabs matched live zero-state routes, and the stale SQLite copy was removed so the UI no longer misstates the current platform-backed registry.

The Phase 726 audit-viewer slice is now browser-proven on the canonical 3001 stack: the page rendered live immutable-audit events, the Authentication filter matched the live actionPrefix=auth. route output, the Chain tab matched the live verify endpoint, and the stats contract now returns chainValid so the Stats tab no longer falsely reports a broken hash chain.

The Phase 726 modules slice is now browser-proven on the canonical 3001 stack: the page rendered live module manifests, marketplace summary, zero-state feature flags, and the live module audit row, and the Entitlements tab no longer crashes because apps/web/src/app/cprs/admin/modules/page.tsx now matches the flat /admin/modules/catalog response shape instead of reading manifest.name from undefined.

The Phase 726 break-glass slice is now browser-proven on the canonical 3001 stack: the page matched the live zero-state and posture routes, a browser-originated request created session 4a203f31-46ae-4dbb-8a2b-cbbef3a7b656, the Sessions tab rendered that pending row truthfully, and the browser deny action returned the tenant to a safe final state with denied:2, active:0, pending:0.

The Phase 726 adapters slice is now browser-proven on the canonical 3001 stack: the page matched live adapter-health and runtime-matrix routes, and apps/web/src/app/cprs/admin/adapters/page.tsx now renders rpcName values truthfully in RPC Coverage so the table no longer shows blank names or duplicate undefined keys under live VEHU data.

The Phase 726 alignment slice is now browser-proven on the canonical 3001 stack: the page matched live score, gates, tripwire, snapshot, and summary routes, and safe browser-driven `Seed Defaults` plus `Capture Now` interactions immediately reflected back through the API with five default tripwires and one new snapshot.

The Phase 726 analytics slice is now browser-proven on the canonical 3001 stack: the page matched live ops, clinical, events, and export-health routes, apps/api/src/services/analytics-store.ts now returns backward-compatible bufferUsage and categories fields so the UI no longer renders NaN% for live buffer metrics, and the browser `Run Aggregation Now` action reflected immediately in the live aggregation bucket counts.

The Phase 726 billing slice is now browser-proven on the canonical 3001 stack: the page now matches the live session-scoped /billing plans, subscription, usage, and health routes, the old nonexistent /admin/billing/* contract is gone from the UI, and apps/api/src/billing/billing-routes.ts now exposes a truthful metering snapshot route so the Usage tab renders real counters instead of failing silently.

The Phase 726 branding slice is now browser-proven on the canonical 3001 stack: the page resolves the active tenant from /admin/my-tenant instead of hardcoding default, the prior cross-tenant 400 loading failure is gone, and the browser Branding, Theme, and Preview tabs now match live /admin/branding/:tenantId and /admin/ui-defaults/:tenantId responses, including a successful safe Save Default Theme round-trip.

The Phase 726 capability-matrix slice is now browser-proven on the canonical 3001 stack: the page matched the live empty payer capability registry without fabricating rows, /rcm/payerops/capability-matrix returned zero cells and zeroed stats, /rcm/payerops/payers also returned zero payers, and the browser empty-state guidance to run ingestion from Payer Directory was truthful on this stack.

The Phase 726 certification slice is now browser-proven on the canonical 3001 stack: the page matches the live /posture/certification payload at 100% / PRODUCTION with 10 passing gates, the Refresh control stays aligned with that route, and apps/web/src/app/cprs/admin/certification/page.tsx no longer crashes on unauthenticated auth-error payloads.

The Phase 726 claims-queue slice is now browser-proven on the canonical 3001 stack: the page matches the live /rcm/claims/lifecycle and /rcm/claims/lifecycle/stats zero-state routes, Refresh stays aligned with those routes, and apps/web/src/app/cprs/admin/claims-queue/page.tsx no longer shows the empty-queue message when the underlying requests have actually failed with authentication errors.

The Phase 726 claims-workbench slice is now browser-proven on the canonical 3001 stack: the page matches the live /rcm/claims/hmo/board, /rcm/claims/source-map, and /rcm/payers/rulepacks routes, apps/web/src/app/cprs/admin/claims-workbench/page.tsx now surfaces unauthenticated failures honestly instead of implying loaded zero-state data, the tab-style warning is removed, and the create form now uses truthful VEHU example values.

The Phase 726 denials slice is now browser-proven on the canonical 3001 stack: the page matches the live /rcm/claims/lifecycle/denials zero-state routes, Refresh stays aligned with the unresolved route, and apps/web/src/app/cprs/admin/denials/page.tsx no longer shows the resolved-empty-state copy when the underlying requests have actually failed with authentication errors.

The Phase 726 payments slice is now browser-proven on the canonical 3001 stack: the page matches the live /payerops/payments/batches, /payerops/payments/reconciliation, /payerops/analytics/aging, and /payerops/payments/underpayments zero-state routes, and apps/web/src/app/cprs/admin/payments/page.tsx now renders truthful load-failure messages on every tab instead of collapsing auth failures into fake empty-state data.

The Phase 726 remittance-intake slice is now browser-proven on the canonical 3001 stack: the page matches the live /rcm/remittance and /rcm/remittance/stats zero-state routes, apps/web/src/app/cprs/admin/remittance-intake/page.tsx now surfaces document and stats auth failures truthfully instead of implying loaded empty remittance data, and the tab-style React warning is removed.

The Phase 726 payer-registry slice is now browser-proven on the canonical 3001 stack: the page matches the live /admin/payers, /admin/payers/stats, and /admin/payers/audit/verify zero-state routes, and apps/web/src/app/cprs/admin/payer-registry/page.tsx now surfaces truthful auth/load failure messaging on registry, evidence, audit, and stats instead of collapsing failures into fake loaded zero-state UI.

The Phase 726 payerops slice is now browser-proven on the canonical 3001 stack: the page matches the live /rcm/payerops/health, /rcm/payerops/stats, /rcm/payerops/enrollments, /rcm/payerops/loa, /rcm/payerops/credentials, and /rcm/payerops/adapters zero-state routes, and apps/web/src/app/cprs/admin/payerops/page.tsx now surfaces truthful auth/load failure messaging instead of misreporting fake OFFLINE status and fake empty payer-operations data.

The Phase 726 performance slice is now browser-proven on the canonical 3001 stack: the page matches the live /admin/performance/summary, /admin/performance/profiles, /admin/performance/budgets, and /admin/performance/slow-queries zero-state routes, and apps/web/src/app/cprs/admin/performance/page.tsx now surfaces truthful auth/load failure messaging instead of collapsing 401s into fake loading or empty-state UI.

The Phase 726 PH market slice is now browser-proven on the canonical 3001 stack: the page matches the live populated /rcm/hmo/market-summary and /rcm/hmo/manifest routes, and apps/web/src/app/cprs/admin/ph-market/page.tsx now uses API_BASE plus strict response validation so auth/load failures render truthful error state instead of crashing on HTML 404s or hanging on unauthenticated loading.

The Phase 726 PhilHealth claims slice is now browser-proven on the canonical 3001 stack: the page matches the live /rcm/philhealth/claims zero-state route, and apps/web/src/app/cprs/admin/philhealth-claims/page.tsx now fails closed on auth/load errors so unauthenticated access no longer masquerades as an empty loaded draft list.

The Phase 726 PhilHealth setup slice is now browser-proven on the canonical 3001 stack: the page matches the live /rcm/philhealth/setup zero-state route, and apps/web/src/app/cprs/admin/philhealth-setup/page.tsx now fails closed on initial load/auth errors instead of rendering a fake blank setup workspace.

The Phase 726 pilot slice is now browser-proven on the canonical 3001 stack: the page matches the live /admin/pilot/sites and /admin/pilot/summary zero-state routes, and apps/web/src/app/cprs/admin/pilot/page.tsx now fails closed on initial load/auth errors instead of rendering the same empty-site and preflight guidance used for a successfully loaded pilot store.

The Phase 726 contracting hub slice is now browser-proven on the canonical 3001 stack: the page matches the live /rcm/hmo/contracting zero-task dashboard route, selecting PH-MAXICARE reveals the truthful empty-task detail, and apps/web/src/app/cprs/admin/contracting-hub/page.tsx now uses API_BASE plus strict response handling instead of crashing on relative-route HTML 404s or hanging on auth failures.

The Phase 726 coverage slice is now browser-proven on the canonical 3001 stack: the page matches the live /admin/coverage score, specialties, gaps, and QA ladder routes, and apps/web/src/app/cprs/admin/coverage/page.tsx now fails closed on auth/load errors instead of crashing the CPRS content boundary with malformed overview state.
